text: Planning a trip is an exciting adventure in itself. When I visited Japan, I spent weeks researching destinations and local customs. Experiencing new cultures was eye-opening, from temple etiquette to bowing greetings. The food was unforgettable—trying authentic ramen from a small street stall or fresh sushi at a market. Language barriers were challenging but fun; I used hand gestures and a translation app to order meals and ask for directions. Despite the awkward moments, these interactions became my best memories. Every mistake taught me something, and now I look back at that journey with warmth, knowing it shaped my love for travel.
